company.name: Brabant Advanced Materials BV
company.description: Mid-market specialty chemicals SME based in Eindhoven (Brainport region), Netherlands. ~€45M revenue, ~95 staff. Supplies high-purity rare-earth catalysts, electronic-grade silane intermediates, and NdPr-based magnetic-material precursors to European automotive electrification, semiconductor, and industrial coatings OEMs.

product_families.name: NdPr concentrate for magnetic materials (NdPr line)

production.primary_location: Eindhoven, Netherlands (Brainport campus; integrated synthesis + purification + packaging)
production.secondary_location: Antwerp port complex, Belgium (toll-finishing line for the eSil family, leased)
production.energy_source: Dutch grid (gas-heavy peakers), 22% solar PPA contracted from 2025 onward; one high-temperature reactor train still gas-fired

input_categories: rare earths and specialty chemicals: lanthanum, cerium, neodymium, praseodymium oxide concentrates
fossil-derived polymers and intermediates: ammonia, ethylene glycol, organic solvents
water-intensive inputs (process): cooling and purification stages
specialty chemicals: high-purity acids, ion-exchange resins

distribution.channel: Direct B2B contracts with EU industrial OEMs

cost_currencies: CNY (occasional spot purchases for NdPr concentrate)
currency_exposure.notable_mismatch: Rare-earth ore feedstock invoiced in USD with secondary CNY exposure for direct Chinese refiners; EUR/USD pair affects REC and NdPr line margins; CNY exposure unhedged

customer_concentration: Top 3 automotive-electrification OEM customers represent ~38% of revenue; no single customer above 20%, but the segment concentration is material
capital_structure_exposure: Rate-sensitive: ~€18M of senior debt with a covenant package refinanced in 2024 at floating rates; 2026 capex plan (~€6M) for solar PPA buildout and reactor electrification is partially debt-financed
labor_exposure: Production and process-engineering labor in Eindhoven (DACH labor market spillover); commercial team in Antwerp and Düsseldorf; specialised process chemists in NL labor market are a constrained resource
unknown_or_uncertain: specific upstream mine / refiner provenance of the NdPr ore concentrate (China-dominant but exact supplier mix unclear; matters for cascade exposure to inland Chinese smelter water/energy constraints)
exposure to upstream Russian natural-gas pricing on EU ammonia and ethylene-glycol feedstocks (suppliers don't disclose gas-cost passthrough)
whether the eSil electronic-grade silane line falls under any specialty-chemicals CBAM scope extension being discussed at the European Commission for 2027
